spec_match.rationale: Listens on `beforeinput` for insertFromDrop and reads dataTransfer — beforeinput already had this pre-v143. The v143 change is that the `input` event also now carries dataTransfer in contenteditable. Page doesn't reference the `input` event anywhere, so the v143 capability isn't exercised.

interactive.rationale: Draggable research-chip palette with a real contenteditable drop target; custom application/x-attribution MIME survives the dataTransfer round-trip; log shows the inferred behaviour.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says 'beforeinput with insertFromDrop now carries dataTransfer' — but dataTransfer was already on beforeinput before v143. The phrasing 'now carries' is wrong; the v143 change is on the input event.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles null dataTransfer ('browser lacks the property' — but this branch is largely unreachable since beforeinput had it for years), and falls through when the custom MIME is absent. Doesn't show what happens to the custom MIME on the `input` event (which would prove v143 specifically).

design_system.rationale: Variables only; no raw hex in script.

openQuestions.title: Move the dataTransfer read to the `input` event
openQuestions.detail: Listen on both `beforeinput` (to preventDefault and assemble the blockquote) AND `input` (to verify the custom MIME survived). The `input`-side log will read 'application/x-attribution: cite-url' on Chrome 143 and 'null' on Chrome 142 — that is the v143 demo. Right now the page would behave identically on both versions.

openQuestions.title: Drop document.execCommand('insertHTML', …)
openQuestions.detail: execCommand is deprecated. Use Range.insertNode with a parsed fragment instead — same end result, modern API, and the resulting subtree is observable through the input event's `getTargetRanges()` for a complete v143 trace.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'drop from outside the page' route
openQuestions.detail: Dragging files or text from another window/app is the most common drop in editors. A drop zone that accepts an OS-level drag (text URL list, file with text content) would prove the dataTransfer plumbing works for cross-origin sources, not just internal chip-to-editor drags.

openQuestions.title: Escape the cite URL before injection
openQuestions.detail: `escapedCite` only replaces &<>. Cite URLs can contain `"` which would break the rendered HTML if it ever ended up inside an attribute (today it doesn't — it's only in text — but the escape table should still match the standard escapeHTML defined in CLAUDE.md rule #4, which also escapes `"` and `'`).

summary: Slickest interaction of the four concepts (chip→editor drag with custom MIME) but listens on beforeinput, not input — wrong half of the spec. Adding an `input` listener that reads the surviving custom MIME would make it the strongest v143 demo of the bunch.
